name: Eddie Durham
abstract: Edward Durham (August 19, 1906 – March 6, 1987) was an American jazz guitarist,  trombonist, composer, and arranger. He was one of the pioneers  of the electric guitar in jazz.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Salute to Pres (Lester Young)

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Grosz
about_quotes.evidence: I remember my first jazz record I ever bought for myself was a Commodore Record called “Paging the Devil” ... by Lester Young, Buck Clayton, and the Kansas City Six I think it was. Eddie Durham.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Grosz interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Al Vollmer
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Durham later on was in the Harlem Blues and Jazz Band for 11 years... I’ll call them American musical geniuses, two exceptional musicians who have gone through the Harlem Blues and Jazz Band. One was Roger Ram Ramirez... and the other was Eddie Durham.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Vollmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, June 8, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: So Eddie Durham had a quartet and I wound up playing in his quartet in a small town up in the Catskills called Goshen, New York... I wound up playing there for years, maybe three or four years with him on the weekends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Jacques Butler
about_quotes.evidence: I became a friend with Eddie Durham and all his brothers, we became very staunch friends. ... me and Eddie Durham was all tight, we hit it off together, we were a team, and we used to play our gigs together

about_quotes.speaker: Count Basie
about_quotes.evidence: "Then in 1935, I enlarged this band at the Reno Club in Kansas City and this eventually became my present organization -- Rushing, Page, Jog, Durham, Ed Lewis and Jack Washington."

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: And all this time Eddie Durham was in the band, Eddie Tompkins, my brother Keg Johnson, and we had one great hell of a band.
